reasoning: "A Minecraft Movie" had a massive start but is running out of steam too early to reach $1 billion. As of May 18, 2025, the film's global total stood at $928.6 million, leaving a gap of $71.4 million. During that May 16-18 weekend, it earned only $8.3 million internationally and around $4 million domestically, creating a worldwide weekend gross of approximately $12 million. The film entered the VOD/digital market in mid-May, which historically suppresses late-stage theatrical legs. Furthermore, all major trailing international markets such as China (April 4), Japan (April 25), and South Korea (April 30) have already been released and their initial box office surges are fully accounted for in the $928.6 million total. Based on standard box-office decay curves (roughly 40-50% drop week-over-week at this stage), a movie earning $12M globally over the weekend will only accumulate around $25-30M more in its remaining run. This places its final global gross trajectory comfortably in the $950M-$960M range, missing the $1 billion mark by $40-50 million. Since the resolution criteria strictly rule out re-releases, and the gap is far too large for a studio accounting strategy to artificially push it over the edge, it is extremely unlikely to resolve YES.
